prompt: Our on-call channel is unusable because the CPU alert flaps — one noisy sample trips it, next sample clears it, forty pages a night. I want a proper smoothed monitor with hysteresis: `ewma_alarm.py`, class AlarmMonitor(alpha, high, low, warmup=0). It keeps an exponentially weighted moving average: first observation seeds it, after that ewma = alpha*value + (1-alpha)*ewma. State is 'ok' or 'alarm': it RAISES when the ewma reaches high (>= high) and only CLEARS once the ewma sinks all the way to low (<= low); anywhere between the two thresholds nothing changes — that band is the whole point. warmup=N means the first N observations can never flip the state (the average is still garbage then), though the ewma itself must update; the check kicks in from observation N on. observe(value) processes one sample and returns the new state. Bookkeeping I need for the dashboard: .state, .value (current ewma, None before any sample), .samples (count), .events — a list of (observation_index, 'raised'|'cleared') tuples, 0-based, accumulated across flaps — and reset() wipes it back to factory (config stays). Construction rules: 0 < alpha <= 1, low < high, warmup a non-negative int, else ValueError. And PLEASE reject bad samples with ValueError before touching any state: NaN, infinities, None, strings — a NaN got averaged in last quarter and the alarm went comatose for a week. test_ewma_alarm.py is the acceptance contract — `python3 test_ewma_alarm.py` must go green without edits to it.
